page: Amazon Web Services m5d.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz, delivering 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes 2400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it supports a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and single-core Geekbench scores fall into the bottom 25 percent tier. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded decompression, and overall Geekbench multi-core metrics sit in the middle 50 percent tier. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in large-block memory bandwidth and Passmark database operations, placing them in the top 25 percent tier.

The m5d.16xlarge offers a balanced resource profile where the inclusion of high-speed local NVMe SSDs provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring temporary, low-latency storage. However, the weak single-core performance represents a clear tradeoff, making it less optimal for single-threaded tasks. Given its strong database operations and average multi-core throughput, this instance is best suited for database hosting, multi-threaded web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 20 Gbps. Benchmark results show a distinct performance profile: while single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression are weak, multi-core processing and multi-threaded decompression remain average. Crucially, the instance excels in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations, ranking in the top 25 percent. This combination of balanced compute-to-memory resources and fast local storage makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for database hosting, multi-threaded web serving, and applications requiring high-speed local scratch space, though it is less suited for single-threaded workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, offering strong database and large-block memory performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining sixty-four virtual cores with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 RAM provide a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency scratch space
Strong large-block memory bandwidth and database operations performance
Average multi-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression capabilities
Weak single-core performance represents a tradeoff for single-threaded tasks
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded web serving, and enterprise applications
